tool_args.label: MTA Code of Ethics Revisions 2023
tool_args.raw_text: The proposed revisions to the MTA All Agency Code of Ethics and the MTA Board Member Code of Ethics are to ensure compliance with the Ethics Commission Reform Act of 2022, to reflect changes in resulting from MTA transformation and to strengthen the MTA Codes of Ethics.

payload.claim_text: The proposed revisions to the MTA All Agency Code of Ethics and the MTA Board Member Code of Ethics are to ensure compliance with the Ethics Commission Reform Act of 2022, to reflect changes in resulting from MTA transformation and to strengthen the MTA Codes of Ethics. Both codes contain mostly non-substantive changes, including changing the name of JCOPE to COELIG.
payload.subject: MTA Code of Ethics

evidence_refs.source_quote: The proposed revisions to the MTA All Agency Code of Ethics and the MTA Board Member Code of Ethics are to ensure compliance with the Ethics Commission Reform Act of 2022, to reflect changes in resulting from MTA transformation and to strengthen the MTA Codes of Ethics.

tool_args.label: Procurement Guidelines Revisions 2023
tool_args.raw_text: Section 2879 of the Public Authorities Law requires the Board to annually review and approve the General Procurement Contract Guidelines and Service Contract Procurement Guidelines.

payload.claim_text: Section 2879 of the Public Authorities Law requires the Board to annually review and approve the General Procurement Contract Guidelines and Service Contract Procurement Guidelines.
payload.subject: Procurement Guidelines

evidence_refs.source_quote: Mr. Kearse informed the Committee that Section 2879 of the Public Authorities Law requires the Board to annually review and approve the General Procurement Contract Guidelines and Service Contract Procurement Guidelines.

tool_args.label: Revised Firearm Rules 2023
tool_args.raw_text: revised firearm rules prohibit the carrying of firearms or other weapons in or on any facility, conveyance, terminal, station, or train except as permitted by law

payload.claim_text: Revised firearm rules prohibit the carrying of firearms or other weapons (as the term is used in the New York State Penal Code) in or on any facility, conveyance, terminal, station, or train except as permitted by law.
payload.subject: MTA Rules of Conduct - Firearm Rules

evidence_refs.source_quote: the revised firearm rules prohibit the carrying of firearms or other weapons (as the term is used in the New York State Penal Code) in or on any facility, conveyance, terminal, station, or train except as permitted by law.

tool_args.label: MTA Audit Committee
tool_args.raw_text: AUDIT COMMITTEE - Charter adopted by the Board Chair and a majority of the members of the Board of the Metropolitan Transportation Authority, as amended on March 21, 2018.

payload.description: Board committee assisting the Board Chair and Board in monitoring financial reporting, internal controls, risk management, and legal/regulatory/ethical compliance

evidence_refs.source_quote: AUDIT COMMITTEE

The Audit Committee shall assist and provide guidance to the Board Chair and the Board in monitoring and overseeing (a) the conduct of the MTA's financial reporting process... (b) the MTA's internal controls and risk management systems; and (c) general matters relating to legal, regulatory and ethical compliance

issues: evidence_refs[1].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer
evidence_refs[1].source_quote is not present in the cited block

tool_args.label: MTA Corporate Governance Committee
tool_args.raw_text: CORPORATE GOVERNANCE COMMITTEE - Charter adopted by the Board Chair and a majority of the members of Board of the Metropolitan Transportation Authority, as amended on March 21, 2018.

payload.description: Board committee assisting the Board Chair and Board in developing governance policies, overseeing corporate governance principles, and reviewing MTA governance

evidence_refs.source_quote: CORPORATE GOVERNANCE COMMITTEE

The Corporate Governance Committee shall assist the Board Chair and the Board in: (i) developing and recommending to the Board, policies to promote honest and ethical conduct... (ii) developing, recommending to the Board and overseeing implementation of MTA policies relating to corporate governance... and (iii) reviewing on a regular basis the overall corporate governance of the MTA

issues: evidence_refs[1].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer
evidence_refs[1].source_quote is not present in the cited block

tool_args.label: MTA Board Committees List
tool_args.raw_text: The Chairman has established the following Committees to assist him and the Board in discharging their responsibilities: (1) the Audit Committee; (2) the Committee on Finance; (3) the Committee on Operations of the New York City Transit Authority, the Manhattan and Bronx Surface Transit Operating Authority, the Staten Island Rapid Transit Operating Authority and the MTA Bus Company; (4) the Committee on Operation of the Metro-North Commuter Railroad; (5) the Committee on Operation of the Long Island Rail Road; (6) the Committee on Operations of the Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority; (7) the Committee on Capital Program Oversight; (8) the Diversity Committee; (9) the Corporate Governance Committee; and (10) the Safety Committee.

payload.claim_text: The Chairman has established the following Committees: (1) Audit Committee; (2) Finance Committee; (3) Committee on Operations of NYCT, MaBSTOA, SIRTOA and MTA Bus; (4) Committee on Operations of Metro-North; (5) Committee on Operations of LIRR; (6) Committee on Operations of TBTA; (7) Capital Program Oversight Committee; (8) Diversity Committee; (9) Corporate Governance Committee; (10) Safety Committee.

tool_args.label: 2024 Corporate Governance Committee Work Plan
tool_args.raw_text: 2024 CORPORATE GOVERNANCE COMMITTEE WORK PLAN

payload.description: Recurring and specific agenda items for the MTA Corporate Governance Committee for 2024, including presentation on corporate governance developments, review of mission statement, procurement guidelines, whistleblower policy, codes of ethics, procurement lobby law compliance, governance principles, by-laws revisions, committee charters, and 2025 work plan approval.

evidence_refs.source_quote: 2024 CORPORATE GOVERNANCE COMMITTEE WORK PLAN

tool_args.label: MTA Governance Guidelines 2023 Revision
tool_args.raw_text: GOVERNANCE GUIDELINES

payload.description: Revisions to the Governance Guidelines to comply with Public Authorities Law Section 1264(1), including gender neutrality, adding Chief Compliance Officer to conflict of interest section, and changing JCOPE to COELIG to comply with the Ethics Commission Reform Act of 2022.
